title: 清洁女工之死
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\209-清洁女工之死-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Hercule Poirot and Inspector Spence discuss various theories regarding the identity of Mrs. McGinty and the motives behind the murder. Poirot suggests that Eve Carpenter, formerly Mrs. Sherlock, may have committed a crime to protect her social standing and her engagement to the ambitious Guy Carpenter; he fears she might be the daughter of the notorious criminal Alfred Craig and would be rejected by Guy if the truth were revealed. Poirot also considers Mrs. Upward a suspect, theorizing she might kill to prevent her son, Robin, from discovering her past identity as Eva Kane. He dismisses the possibility of Robin killing to protect his mother, instead suggesting Robin is too selfish and might exploit such information for his own benefit. Spence notes that the destruction of records during the war makes verifying these identities difficult.

While waiting for a train at Gilchester station, Poirot is suddenly and forcefully pushed toward the tracks by an unknown person. He is saved from being crushed by an oncoming train by a tall, burly army sergeant. Viewing this assassination attempt as confirmation that his investigation is on the right track, Poirot calls Superintendent Spence to report the incident. Meanwhile, at the Carpenter residence, a meter reader and Guy Carpenter's butler discuss the upcoming elections and mention that Mr. Carpenter drives a Bentley.
